title: Incorporate Pyright into Python validation
summary: Wired Pyright into Python validation as a separate make pyright gate covering automation/ and Tools/. Configured for typeCheckingMode='basic' with reportPossiblyUnboundVariable / reportUndefinedVariable / reportMissingImports at error severity; reportArgumentType / reportOptionalSubscript / reportAssignmentType at warning. Fixed one production code issue: Tools/localization-review-export.py.fail typed as -> NoReturn so Pyright knows it never returns, eliminating two reportPossiblyUnboundVariable errors. Baseline: 0 errors / 23 warnings. Queued a follow-up slice (automation-pyright-tighten-severities) to clean up the warnings and re-tighten the three downgraded severities back to error.
files_changed: automation/queue/slices.json (mark this slice done + add automation-pyright-tighten-severities)
not_claimed: all Python code is type-correct (23 warnings remain)
make pyright is folded into make automation-check (it stays a separate gate)
no Python type bugs exist (warnings cover real type-correctness concerns; downgraded for an honest baseline)
residual_risk: 23 baseline warnings, mostly test-time None defaults typed as non-Optional. Real but mostly benign; queued under automation-pyright-tighten-severities for cleanup.
pyrightconfig.json downgrades three severities. The cleanup follow-up should flip them back to error. Until then, this class of bug only surfaces as a warning.
make pyright is not part of make automation-check or make verify by default. CI / agents must invoke it explicitly. This is intentional per the slice notes.
